items.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
items.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

items.title: Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosted leaders of European NATO powers in Berlin to hash out consensus on bolstering European defense. With US tensions looming, the leaders signaled that transatlantic ties are improving.

items.title: Trump airs grievances with NATO chief over alliance's lack of Iran war support
items.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.

items.title: Ukraine recovery summit opens in Poland without Zelensky
items.description: A landmark conference on Ukraine's post-war reconstruction opened Thursday in Poland without Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, who is snubbing the event amid a diplomatic row with ally Warsaw.

items.title: As temperatures soar, France's TotalEnergies awaits ruling in climate trial
items.description: A day after France hit record high temperatures, a court in Paris is set to rule Thursday on a landmark climate change case that could see energy giant TotalEnergies forced to reduce its oil and gas production. The lawsuit, brought by a group of NGOs and the city of Paris, argues the French corporation is violating a 2017 law that requires companies to prevent human rights abuses and environmental risks. It is the first time that the so-called corporate duty of vigilance law is being applied to climate change.

items.title: US to create genomic and cellular 'BioVault' for endangered species
EU targets Amazon, Microsoft cloud units for Big Tech “gatekeeper” rules

items.description: From what to wear to getting a restful night's sleep, you share your tips for beating the heat.

items.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
items.description: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

items.title: Find out which university degrees could earn you most across your lifetime
items.description: New data suggests which university degrees have the highest and lowest financial returns over a lifetime.

items.title: Truck stuck for almost five minutes before it was hit by express train
items.description: An ATSB investigation found a truck was stuck on a Brisbane level crossing for almost five minutes before being hit by a train.

items.description: On stage, performers transformed ordinary life experiences into spontaneous theatre, drawing smiles, reflection, and sometimes sadness from the audience

items.description: Elected to represent the Liberal heartland seat of Nedlands just last year, Jonathan Huston announces he is quitting the party.

items.description: Rubio's Gulf tour is the first high-level diplomatic mission since the US-Iran framework agreement last week.

items.description: President Putin has hinted at peace talks as the economy fails, people flee cities and toxic 'oil rains' fall.

items.title: Oil prices back to pre-war levels on rising Middle East supply
items.description: The price of Brent crude has reached its lowest since February 27, before the war started.

items.description: Kenyan police put on a massive show of force Thursday in a bid to prevent protests marking two years since a landmark Gen Z uprising

items.title: The science behind the back-to-back earthquakes that struck northern Venezuela
items.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with earthquake scientist Judith Hubbard of Cornell University about the science behind the multiple earthquakes in Venezuela, Japan and northern California Wednesday.

items.title: NT outlaw bikie gang president sentenced to 13 years in prison
items.description: Secretly taped phone calls have captured the former president of the Mongols bikie gang's Northern Territory chapter discussing interstate drug deals while helping supply methamphetamine worth almost $800,000 to customers across the Top End.

items.title: World Cup 2026: More MENA countries competing than ever before
items.description: More Middle East and North Africa (MENA) countries are competing in the 2026 World Cup than at any previous tournament, much to the delight of fans from Morocco to Jordan. But as always with sports, politics is never far from the pitch. Iran, of course, springs to mind. Never before has a country hosted a World Cup while at war with one of the competing nations.

items.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
items.description: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

items.description: The groups said eyewitnesses gave account of how Mr Sowore was allegedly dragged and manhandled by security personnel in the presence of lawyers, supporters and members of the public within the court premises. The post CISLAC, HEDA condemn assault of Sowore, Kaduna mob killing appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: MSCI upgrades Bulgaria to frontier; Vietnam, Argentina unchanged
items.description: MSCI indicated no changes to Argentina’s classification as a Standalone Market, effectively keeping the country out of its global stock-market indexes. Leer más

items.title: Europe's heatwave shifts east as France raises health alert to highest level

items.title: India: Passport not proof of citizenship, ministry says, sparking social media debate
items.description: The Ministry of External Affairs has said that the passport does not prove citizenship in India. People are now asking on social media how else they can prove that they are Indians. DW has the latest.

items.title: IRGC warns against new Hormuz route for ships: What we know
items.description: Experts say disagreements over shipping routes and transit fees could complicate efforts to reach a permanent agreement.

items.title: Australia teen social media ban has little impact: research
items.description: Underage users have been dodging the restrictions by using accounts registered to older people, setting up fake accounts, or by logging into private browsers

items.title: Lack of basic services leads South Africans to look beyond traditional party allegiance
items.description: Pot holes, garbage and sewage - all challenges facing the residents of South Africa’s Evaton West township. Many voters cite the lack of basic services as the reason they abandoned the ruling ANC, handing victory to the opposition Democratic Alliance in last month.

items.title: France's health system under pressure as heatwave continues
items.description: As France and Europe continues to swelter under scorching temperatures, France's healthcare system has been left struggling to cope, with Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu announcing on Thursday that the country's health alert warning system was being raised to its highest level. France 24's Olivia Bizot has the details.

items.title: Family seeks justice after Indian sailor killed in US strike
items.description: After a US strike on a commercial vessel in the Gulf of Oman killed an Indian sailor, there are urgent questions about seafarer safety, accountability and India's response. DW spoke with the sailor's family.

items.title: Sexual violence used as a 'weapon of war' since start of Sudan conflict, UN says
items.description: Sexual violence has being used “consistently as a tactic and weapon of war” since the conflict in Sudan erupted three years ago. That’s the finding of a new report by the United Nations Human Rights Office.

items.title: UK swelters in hottest June day on record
items.description: The UK recorded its hottest ever June temperature on Wednesday with the mercury rising to 36.1C in southern England, breaking the previous record of 35.6C set in 1976. It is the second month in a row that Britain has broken historical temperature records, as scientists warn that climate change is making extreme weather events like heatwaves more frequent and intense.

items.title: U.S.-Iran deal may leave Netanyahu as biggest casualty: analysts
items.description: “Not only did he lose the war with Iran, ​he has also lost Trump as a friend. He is now isolated not only ‌internationally, but locked in a major dispute with Trump,” a former Netanyahu adviser says

items.title: Poland, Ukraine strike conciliatory tone at reconstruction conference
items.description: A landmark conference on Ukraine's post-war reconstruction opened on Thursday in Poland without President Volodymyr Zelensky, who is snubbing the event over a diplomatic row with ally Warsaw and weeks of squabbling over historical differences. Kyiv sent Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko instead who struck a friendly tone and didn't mention the historical disagreements.

items.title: Ebola outbreak 'serious' but not 'out of control', head of Africa CDC says
items.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Dr Jean Kaseya, head of the Africa CDC, said the Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo is "serious" but not "out of control". He warned that "no one is safe" if it is not stopped at the source and argued that the West would already have a vaccine if the disease had struck there.

items.title: Vinii, Father of the House of Revlon: 'Voguing is about liberation and freedom'
items.description: The King of the Paris Voguing scene tells FRANCE 24 how the art form is about your own liberation and freedom. Voguing has been widely championed in France by the LGBTQ+ community. Originating in the 1980s from the 1960s Harlem ballroom scene, the dance combines fashion, posing and self-expression. In Perspective, we speak to the Father of the international House of Revlon, Vinii.

items.title: Why is Europe the fastest-warming continent?
items.description: Extreme heat has been smashing temperature records across Europe: in Spain, the United Kingdom and in France. A persistent high pressure system known as a heat dome has settled above the continent for the second time in a month, trapping hot air in like a lid on a pot. Scientists say extreme heat is being driven by climate change, but what makes Europe so vulnerable? Eliza Herbert and France 2 take a look at why it is the fastest-warming continent.

items.title: Peru's Keiko Fujimori celebrates election win as opponent alleges fraud
items.description: Apparent Peruvian election winner Keiko Fujimori on Wednesday said her victory was all but "irreversible" while acknowledging her country's political splinters. The conservative presidential candidate is poised to win the tightly fought leadership contest after she consolidated an unassailable lead over her leftist rival Roberto Sanchez late Tuesday. Sanchez said he would not recognise a government headed by Fujimori, claiming a "serious violation of the electoral process".

items.description: Defence lawyers in the trial of Central African Republic’s former president, François Bozizé, on Tuesday denounced what they say are procedural irregularities.

items.title: Oil price falls to pre-war levels as ships being moving through Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Brent crude was trading at around $72 a barrel on Thursday morning, the price it was at the day before the launch of attacks on Iran.

items.title: Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations condemned
items.description: South Australian Liberal senator Alex Antic has been roundly condemned over comments he made about vaccinations after avian influenza was found on Australia's southern coast.

items.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
items.description: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

items.title: Republicans have 'growing litany list' against Trump
items.description: US President Donald ​Trump faced pointed criticism over the Iran war on Wednesday in a closed-door meeting with fellow Republicans, with Trump reportedly engaging in a shouting match with Republican Senator Bill Cassidy. It was another sign, says France 24's Douglas Herbert, of growing dissatisfaction with the president among some Republicans.

items.title: Oman opens temporary Strait of Hormuz shipping routes, says no tolls will be charged
items.description: In ‌coordination with the International Maritime Organization, Oman established temporary maritime corridors to help ships leave the area safely amid heightened security risks

items.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
items.description: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now the Bundestag is debating a change.

items.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
items.description: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

items.title: South Africa's appeals court grants Lungu's family burial right
items.description: A legal battle over where former Zambian President Edgar Lungu's remains will be buried may be over, more than a year after he died, as South Africa's Supreme Court of Appeal ruled Tuesday in favor of his family and rejected the Zambian government's claim of custody over his body.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: World Cup blues in Messi and Milei’s Argentina
items.description: The World Cup provides partial reprieve for nearly six weeks, especially if Messi & Co are in it for a deep run. Politics, though, continues its course, and Adorni is coming up to the chopping block. Leer más

items.title: Trump abruptly cancels signing Housing bill, blindsiding Republicans
items.description: The President declared on social media that he now wants Congress to first pass a bill that would mandate stricter rules for voter identification in federal elections

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: At least 164 dead, 971 injured in quakes, says Delcy Rodriguez
items.description: Acting Venezuela President warned the toll was expected to rise as rescuers searched collapsed buildings and emergency crews reached devastated areas

items.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
items.description: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

items.title: Greetings from sweltering Switzerland
items.description: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.

items.title: Argentina’s economy grew more than expected in first quarter
items.description: Gross domestic product expanded 0.7% compared to the previous quarter, above analyst forecasts, even as unemployment rose and wages trailed inflation. Leer más

items.title: India’s envoy to Bangladesh Dinesh Trivedi assumes office, announces resumption of travel visas
items.description: Visa applications can be submitted starting June 28, Mr. Trivedi said after presenting his credentials to Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin; Union govt. accords Mr. Trivedi the status of a Union Minister for ceremonial functions

items.title: Amazon to invest additional $13 billion in India cloud, AI
Heat and drinks breaks help smaller teams at World Cup, says Argentina coach Scaloni
items.description: Hydration breaks are helping the smaller teams at the World Cup, Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said Sunday. Leer más

items.title: Ethiopian journalists win US’ democracy award for courage
items.description: One of the last remaining independent media outlets in Ethiopia, Addis Standard, has won the US' National Endowment for Democracy award for its journalists' resilience and courage working under a repressive media landscape. Addis Standard will travel to Washington DC in September to accept the award. Ethiopia is the country that jails the most journalists in Sub-Saharan Africa only after Eritrea. Topics like armed conflict and human rights abuses have led local journalists to be imprisoned and international correspondents to be expelled from the country. Our correspondent in Ethiopia Tom Canetti sat down with the editor-in-chief of Addis Standard Yonas Kedir.

items.title: France's Macron hosts Italy's Meloni after Trump rift
items.description: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, fresh from a spat with US leader Donald Trump, was set Thursday to meet French President Emmanuel Macron in the resort of Antibes on the French Riviera on Thursday to tighten ties, including deeper cooperation on defence and energy.

items.title: Jesica Cirio's dollar videos rock case against ex-Buenos Aires Province official Martín Insaurralde
items.description: Images of the model displaying stacks of cash in the home she once shared with veteran Kirchnerite politician Martín Insaurralde have put the courts’ corruption file back on centre stage. Authorities seek to determine whether the former official’s assets can be explained by his declared income. Leer más

items.title: The woman who fought for Pakistan's disappeared men now faces life in jail
items.description: The Nobel Peace Prize nominee has spent years demanding answers about the missing in Balochistan province.

items.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
items.description: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

items.title: Angolan national airline starts a weekly service to Guangzhou in China
items.description: The re-launch of the non-stop passenger route comes at TAAG implements a fleet modernisation and expansion plan.

items.title: Dollar eases from 13-month high after data burst
More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
items.description: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

items.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
items.description: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

items.title: Israel gets thrown under the bus
items.description: Unlike the US president and his business cronies, the Israelis have no illusions about the fanatical Iranian regime. Leer más

items.title: Exports boom but households struggle as INDEC posts 2.3% growth
items.description: GDP rose 2.3% in the first quarter of 2026 year-on-year, driven by exports and extractive industries, but unemployment is up, bank defaults have hit a 20-year high and manufacturing is contracting. Leer más

items.title: Europe heatwave is 'price to pay for fossil fuel pollution': UN climate chief
items.description: Until humanity stops burning colossal amounts of coal, oil and gas, extreme heat will keep getting worse, says UN climate chief Simon Stiell

items.title: South Africa write history while Neymar makes first appearance
items.description: After a disastrous start at the World Cup, Bafana Bafana have qualified for the knockout stage for the first-ever time. The squad beat South Korea 1-0 in their last group stage game to reach the Round of 32. Meanwhile superstar Neymar came in as a substitute for Brazil to the delight of fans, as the team took top spot of Group C by beating Scotland 3-0.

items.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
items.description: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

items.title: Trump ally wins Colombia election, heralding pro-market turn
items.description: Election of conservative lawyer heralds swing back to business-friendly and pro-US policies after four years of leftist rule under Gustavo Petro. Leer más

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand
items.description: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

items.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
items.description: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

items.title: EU joins US-led 'Pax Silica' on securing AI, chip supply chains
Venezuela hit by two powerful earthquakes
items.description: Strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela's capital just seconds apart, bringing down buildings in Caracas.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Haitians reflect on first World Cup experience in 52 years
items.description: Haitian diaspora reflects after Haiti participated in the World Cup for the first time in 52 years.

items.title: PDP faction disowns Pantami, names Gombe governorship candidate
items.description: This comes weeks after the Nyesom Wike-led faction announced former Minister of Communications and Digital Economy, Isa Pantami, as the PDP flagbearer in the state. The post PDP faction disowns Pantami, names Gombe governorship candidate appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Venezuela’s deadly history of earthquakes — and how they affected its politics
items.description: As the 1812 earthquake during the War of Independence illustrated, Venezuela is no stranger to natural disasters being bound up with moments of political upheaval
